subject: [COMMON] Fix condition bracket bashisms - step 1
commit_message:
[COMMON] Fix condition bracket bashisms - step 1

pointed out by checkbashisms

$ mycmd=$(tox -e checkbashisms
| grep '(\[\[ foo \]\] should be \[ foo \])'
| sed -e "s@^[^.]*\(.[^ ]*\) line \([0-9]*\) .*@sed -i '\2s/\\\[\\\[\\\(
[^]]*\\\)\\\]\\\]/[\\\1]/g' \1;@")

$ eval $mycmd

plus fix manually quoting hells induced and bash specific regex
and multi-conditions

Issue-ID: OOM-2643
Signed-off-by: Guillaume Lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: Ie7ca5b71938fae22c200b7fead418618160fbe19

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> @Sylvain
> For a second time in a row gating got terribly broken on this patch. Do we have some issues with gating or it must be related to this patch?

I would say likely the second option.
Double brackets replacements may have tricky side-effects and as a newbie contributor to oom, I was misled by too much trust in verified +1. It seems it is not a good idea to rely on gate votes here.
I made my possible to segment this whole contribution into small independent commits and there are many commits less trickier to interpret, e..g. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/119148
I propose to rebase some of them and see if they behave better.

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> > Patch Set 7:
> > 
> > @Sylvain
> > For a second time in a row gating got terribly broken on this patch. Do we have some issues with gating or it must be related to this patch?
> 
> I would say likely the second option.
> Double brackets replacements may have tricky side-effects and as a newbie contributor to oom, I was misled by too much trust in verified +1. It seems it is not a good idea to rely on gate votes here.
> I made my possible to segment this whole contribution into small independent commits and there are many commits less trickier to interpret, e..g. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/119148
> I propose to rebase some of them and see if they behave better.

Just for your reference the +verify that you get from Jenkins is just checking if helm charts are linting correctly there is absolutely no checks if any of the shell scripts works fine.

But we have a second gate (ONAP JobDeployer) which really tries to deploy onap and run those scripts and that's the only trustworthy test in your case

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> > Patch Set 7:
> > 
> > @Sylvain
> > For a second time in a row gating got terribly broken on this patch. Do we have some issues with gating or it must be related to this patch?
> 
> I would say likely the second option.
> Double brackets replacements may have tricky side-effects and as a newbie contributor to oom, I was misled by too much trust in verified +1. It seems it is not a good idea to rely on gate votes here.
> I made my possible to segment this whole contribution into small independent commits and there are many commits less trickier to interpret, e..g. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/119148
> I propose to rebase some of them and see if they behave better.

well I guess that the patch has side effects yes.
please split it into smaller chunks to see if it's OK

FYI, I'm not really confident here because most of these scripts are almost never used and so we may break them without noticing :(

on the list, only deploy and undeploy are used for example
all the other ones are not tested during verify or gate so I don't know if it's a good idea to remove the bashisms (well, I'd like to but someone has to test...)

Patch Set 8:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1
> 
> > Patch Set 7:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 7:
> > > 
> > > @Sylvain
> > > For a second time in a row gating got terribly broken on this patch. Do we have some issues with gating or it must be related to this patch?
> > 
> > I would say likely the second option.
> > Double brackets replacements may have tricky side-effects and as a newbie contributor to oom, I was misled by too much trust in verified +1. It seems it is not a good idea to rely on gate votes here.
> > I made my possible to segment this whole contribution into small independent commits and there are many commits less trickier to interpret, e..g. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/119148
> > I propose to rebase some of them and see if they behave better.

> well I guess that the patch has side effects yes.

> FYI, I'm not really confident here because most of these scripts are almost never used and so we may break them without noticing :(

> on the list, only deploy and undeploy are used for example
> all the other ones are not tested during verify or gate so I don't know if it's a good idea to remove the bashisms (well, I'd like to but someone has to test...)

Fair enough, I am rebasing and splitting this to send you another proposal.
Feel free to propose me a different segmentation.
I think the problem was coming from multiple conditions that are interpreted differently inside simple and double brackets.
Let's see if it behaves better once this problem fixed.
